name: Greek entity cronus
shortDescription: Cronus was the leader of the Titans and the father of many Olympian gods. He overthrew his father Uranus and was later overthrown by his son Zeus.
description: Cronus (also spelled Kronos or Cronos) was a significant figure in Greek mythology, primarily known as the leader of the Titans. He was the son of Uranus (the Sky) and Gaia (the Earth). Driven by Gaia's resentment of Uranus's tyrannical rule and his imprisonment of their children, Cronus castrated his father with a sickle, thus separating the Sky from the Earth and becoming the ruler of the cosmos. This act ushered in the Golden Age, a period of peace and prosperity. However, Cronus, fearing a prophecy that he would be overthrown by his own son, swallowed each of his children as they were born. His wife, Rhea, hid their youngest child, Zeus, and tricked Cronus into swallowing a stone instead. Zeus eventually grew up, overthrew Cronus, and freed his siblings from his father's stomach. Cronus and the Titans then fought a ten-year war against the Olympian gods, known as the Titanomachy, which the Olympians ultimately won. Cronus was then imprisoned in Tartarus, though some accounts suggest he later ruled over the Isles of the Blessed. Cronus is often associated with time, particularly in later interpretations, although this association is not prominent in early Greek mythology. His role as the father of the Olympian gods and his overthrow of Uranus mark him as a pivotal figure in the transition from the primordial deities to the Olympian pantheon.
